rule_statement: Continuation of schemes within financial powers requires appraisal and approval per existing guidelines
ministry_must_show: Compliance with appraisal and approval process

rule_statement: Appraisal/approval authority based on the period 2021-22 to 2025-26
ministry_must_show: Authority determination based on specified years

rule_statement: Ongoing schemes with sunset dates before 2021-22 are exempt from the above rules
ministry_must_show: Documentation of sunset dates for exemptions

rule_statement: Complete appraisal/approval before 31-03-2021, considering third-party evaluations
ministry_must_show: Evidence of appraisal completion and evaluations

rule_statement: Proposals for schemes over Rs. 500 crore require EFC appraisal and approval
ministry_must_show: Submit detailed proposal for EFC review

rule_statement: No standalone CSS or umbrella scheme should have outlay below Rs. 300 crore over five years
Each scheme under an umbrella must have an outlay of at least Rs. 25 crore for five years
Schemes with outlay less than Rs. 25 crore should be classified as projects or pilots
Proposals must show convergence with similar schemes to avoid overlap
Avoid unnecessary establishment and administrative expenses; focus on resource convergence
Common norms should be followed across similar schemes for better coordination
Proposals must include Aadhaar authentication for beneficiary identification and fund disbursement
Incorporate cashless and electronic transactions in scheme design
Specify the name of the Umbrella scheme for continuation
ministry_must_show: Name of the relevant Umbrella scheme

rule_statement: Provide total outlay of the umbrella scheme, including scheme-wise and year-wise breakdown
ministry_must_show: Outlay details with Central and State shares

rule_statement: Identify central components fully funded by the central government and state components shared with states/UTs
ministry_must_show: Detailed breakdown of funding sources for components

rule_statement: Submit evaluation report with major findings and comments on observations
Propose major changes from earlier scheme with justification
Report any major changes in costing norms
ministry_must_show: Details of any new costing norms

rule_statement: Outline convergence architecture with other central schemes
Provide rationale for scheme continuation
Propose year-wise output/outcomes
Specify sunset date for the scheme
Detail posts created for the scheme with financial implications
ministry_must_show: List of posts and financial impact

rule_statement: Propose any additional posts with financial implications
Include comments from stakeholders and responses in tabular form
ministry_must_show: Tabular format of comments and responses

rule_statement: Ministry/Department must provide comments on Third-Party Evaluation findings and attach the evaluation copy
ministry_must_show: Comments on evaluation findings and evaluation report

rule_statement: Details of components being dropped, merged, modified, or newly added must be provided with justifications
Year-wise outlay for the proposed scheme must be detailed for specified years
Actual expenditure for the last 4 years must be reported along with eligible outlay calculation
ministry_must_show: Expenditure data and eligible outlay calculation

rule_statement: Fund sharing ratio between Centre and States must be uniform unless justified otherwise
ministry_must_show: Justification for differing ratios if applicable

rule_statement: Any changes in cost norms compared to the existing scheme must be justified
ministry_must_show: Details of cost norm changes and justifications

rule_statement: Every Central Scheme must have a sunset clause with proposed interventions described
Details of posts created for the existing scheme and proposed additional posts must be provided
ministry_must_show: Details of existing and proposed posts with financial implications

rule_statement: Diagrams of scheme implementation structure and fund flow must be provided
Ministry/Department must provide comments on Third-Party Evaluation findings and attach the evaluation copy
ministry_must_show: Comments on evaluation findings and evaluation report

rule_statement: No new Scheme or Sub-Scheme without prior in-principle approval from DoE, except Budget Speech announcements
Budget Estimates must align with approved scheme architecture; deviations need prior agreement with DoE
ministry_must_show: Agreement from DoE for any deviations

rule_statement: Ministries must merge, restructure, or drop redundant schemes; in-principle approval not needed for this
DoE can merge, restructure, or drop schemes to improve efficiency, in consultation with concerned departments
Quality of scheme/project formulation is crucial to avoid execution issues; invest time in this stage
New Schemes require a Concept Paper and stakeholder consultations; ongoing schemes need rationalization
Project preparation must start with a Feasibility Report to confirm techno-economic viability
No new Company or Body can be set up without Cabinet approval, regardless of outlay
Delegation of powers for appraisal and approval of Original Cost Estimates varies by cost thresholds
ministry_must_show: Compliance with appraisal and approval limits

rule_statement: Financial Advisers can refer financial matters to DoE; mandatory for proposals above Rs. 300 crore
ministry_must_show: Inclusion of DoE in SFC/DIB meetings for large proposals

rule_statement: Delegated powers can only be used if budget allocation or medium-term outlay is approved by DoE
ministry_must_show: Confirmation of budgetary allocation from DoE

rule_statement: Ministries/Departments must rigorously examine proposals in project design and delivery
ministry_must_show: Evidence of thorough project review process

rule_statement: Attention to recurring liabilities and fund availability post adjustment of committed liabilities is essential
ministry_must_show: Assessment of recurring liabilities and fund status

rule_statement: Separate orders from Department of Economic Affairs apply for PPP project appraisal and approval
ministry_must_show: Reference to applicable orders for PPP projects

rule_statement: Cost increases up to 20% due to certain factors approved by Secretary with Financial Adviser's concurrence
ministry_must_show: Concurrence from Financial Adviser for cost increase

rule_statement: Increases beyond 20% require review by Revised Cost Committee before further approval
ministry_must_show: Submission to Revised Cost Committee for reasons and recommendations

rule_statement: Pre-investment activities up to Rs. 100 crore can be approved by Secretary with Financial Adviser's concurrence
ministry_must_show: Financial resources availability and in-principle approval

rule_statement: Pre-investment activities above Rs. 100 crore must follow full appraisal and approval procedure
ministry_must_show: Compliance with appraisal and approval procedures

rule_statement: All schemes must have a sunset date and undergo outcome review at the end of the Twelfth Plan period
ministry_must_show: Resubmission of schemes for appraisal and approval

rule_statement: Output-outcome framework for schemes must be prepared with NITI Aayog's approval
Extension of schemes across Finance Commission Cycles contingent on evaluation results
Define measurable outcomes for each scheme for the medium term until 2019-20
Budget allocation for 2016-17 should project a normative increase of 5-10% annually until 2019-20
Yearly physical outputs must align with projected financial resources and measurable outcomes
ministry_must_show: Demonstrate consistency with financial and outcome metrics

rule_statement: Output-outcome framework must be approved by CEO, NITI Aayog by 30 June 2016
ministry_must_show: Submission of approved framework by deadline

rule_statement: Each scheme must have an evaluation framework; continuation beyond the Fourteenth Finance Commission depends on evaluation results
ministry_must_show: Submit evaluation framework and results to NITI Aayog

rule_statement: Identify outcome parameters as indicators of desired outcomes; prioritize for upcoming budgets

rule_statement: Beneficiary-oriented schemes must specify if they are DBT schemes and detail beneficiary identification via Aadhaar authentication

rule_statement: Funds must be disbursed through APBS using the DBT scheme code

rule_statement: EFC/SFC proposals for new schemes must use the revised format and include required enclosures
Obtain in-principle approval from DoE before initiating any new scheme or sub-scheme
Consult NITi Aayog for new schemes as part of the approval process

rule_statement: EFC/SFC proposals or new schemes must be submitted after 15.11.2023

ministry_must_show: Complete summary sheet as per format

ministry_must_show: Table of approvals with agency and availability

rule_statement: Administrative structure and manpower requirements must be detailed

rule_statement: Monitoring systems and audit arrangements must be specified
ministry_must_show: Details of monitoring and audit systems

rule_statement: Third-party evaluations must be arranged with specified methodologies
No new scheme or sub-scheme initiated without prior in-principle approval from DoE, except Budget Speech announcements
ministry_must_show: Attach DoE in-principle approval with proposals

rule_statement: Ministries must stop budgetary support for schemes initiated without DoE approval

rule_statement: Concept Paper must be prepared considering project feasibility through stakeholder consultations

rule_statement: Every scheme must have a sunset date for continuation based on outcome review
Schemes must align with the financial resource cycles of Central and State governments
ministry_must_show: Evidence of alignment with Finance Commission cycles

rule_statement: Schemes ending on 31.03.2026 must undergo appraisal for continuation in the next FC cycle
ministry_must_show: Outcome review results and appraisal memos

rule_statement: Central Sector Schemes require Third Party Evaluation as per issued ToRs
Appraisal memos must be submitted to DoE by 31.10.2025
Schemes with outlay up to Rs 500 Crore can be approved by Administrative Ministry as per DoE guidelines
Appraisal/approval must consider third-party evaluation reports

rule_statement: Submit a list of all approved schemes to DoE in prescribed format
Schemes with outlay above Rs 500 Crore require appraisal and approval through EFC
ministry_must_show: Submit appraisal documents as per DoE OM No. 24(35)/PF-II/2012

rule_statement: No CSS should have a financial outlay below Rs. 300 crore for five years
Financial outlay for CSs must be at least Rs. 50 crore over five years
Central Sector Schemes under Rs 100 Crore should achieve purpose in a cycle, else need evaluation for continuation
If continued, schemes must undergo appraisal/approval as per DoE OM 24(35)/PF-II/2012

rule_statement: Total projected outlay for a continuing scheme should not exceed 5.5 times the average annual expenditure

rule_statement: Ministries can request more funds for a scheme if they reduce another scheme's budget with justification
All schemes must operate within the approved outlay limits over the FC cycle

rule_statement: Outlay for saturation schemes based on estimated number of beneficiaries; sanctions limited to approved outlay

rule_statement: Carry forward committed expenditure within approved outlay to next FC cycle allowed

rule_statement: Seek specific approval for increased outlay if beneficiaries exceed projections during scheme duration

rule_statement: Funding pattern for CSS must align with NITI Aayog's specified OMs from 2016 and 2019

rule_statement: Ministries must respond quickly to draft Evaluation Report from NITI Aayog
Continuation approval requires a positive evaluation report demonstrating effectiveness and ongoing need

rule_statement: A scheme may continue as is or with modifications based on evaluation findings

rule_statement: Schemes must maintain consistent funding patterns across components unless justified otherwise
Funds must be disbursed through AEPS for Aadhaar authentication, not just seeding
Inter-component resource transfer allowed but must have a ceiling to prevent overspending

Recalibrate, restructure, or close schemes based on evaluations and effectiveness
Proposals must show convergence with similar schemes to avoid overlap
Examine ongoing schemes for potential merger or dropping due to overlap
